pairs.mechanism: Senolytic combination; quercetin is a mild CYP3A4 inhibitor and may marginally increase dasatinib exposure. Clinically acceptable in single-dose / monthly pulsed protocols.
pairs.recommendation: Acceptable as intermittent senolytic pulse (Tkemaladze & Apkhazava 2019; Tkemaladze 2023). Avoid continuous daily co-administration; monitor CBC and LFTs if repeated.

pairs.mechanism: Additive bleeding risk: NSAID inhibits platelet aggregation and can cause GI mucosal injury while warfarin impairs coagulation.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid routine co-administration. If NSAID is essential, prefer shortest course + PPI; increase INR monitoring frequency.

pairs.mechanism: Additive antiplatelet + anticoagulant effect.
pairs.recommendation: Combine only for specific cardiology indications (e.g. mechanical valve + ACS) with close INR and bleeding surveillance.

pairs.mechanism: Vitamin K directly antagonises warfarin's mechanism.
pairs.recommendation: Keep dietary/supplemental vitamin K intake stable. Avoid supplemental doses unless reversing over-anticoagulation.

pairs.mechanism: St John's wort induces CYP2C9 and CYP3A4, lowering warfarin plasma levels and INR — thrombosis risk.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, monitor INR weekly.

pairs.mechanism: Ginkgo inhibits platelet-activating factor; additive bleeding.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid routine combination; monitor for bruising/bleeding.

pairs.mechanism: High-dose fish oil (>3 g/d) may prolong bleeding time.
pairs.recommendation: Doses <=1 g/d generally safe; above that, monitor INR and bleeding.

pairs.mechanism: Garlic has mild antiplatelet activity; additive bleeding.
pairs.recommendation: Limit high-dose garlic extracts; monitor INR.

pairs.mechanism: High-dose vitamin E (>400 IU/d) can potentiate anticoagulation.
pairs.recommendation: Keep vitamin E <400 IU/d; monitor INR if higher.

pairs.mechanism: Serotonin syndrome via additive serotonergic activity.
pairs.recommendation: Contraindicated. Observe washout: >=14 d off MAOI before SSRI; >=5 wk off fluoxetine before MAOI.

pairs.mechanism: Serotonin syndrome; fluoxetine has long half-life.
pairs.recommendation: Contraindicated. Require >=5 weeks washout.

pairs.mechanism: Serotonin syndrome.
pairs.recommendation: Contraindicated. >=14 d washout both directions.

pairs.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effect; lowered seizure threshold.
pairs.recommendation: Prefer non-serotonergic analgesic; if combined, low dose + monitor.

pairs.mechanism: Additive serotonergic activity → serotonin syndrome.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid combination.

pairs.mechanism: Hyperkalaemia: ACEi reduces aldosterone-mediated K+ excretion.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid K+ supplements unless hypokalaemic; monitor serum K+.

pairs.mechanism: Additive hyperkalaemia (both retain K+).
pairs.recommendation: Monitor K+ and creatinine every 1–2 wk after initiation.

pairs.mechanism: NSAID blunts ACEi antihypertensive effect; risk of AKI.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid chronic NSAID; if essential, monitor BP + creatinine.

pairs.mechanism: NSAID blunts ACEi antihypertensive effect; risk of AKI.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid chronic NSAID; if essential, monitor BP + creatinine.

pairs.mechanism: Amiodarone raises digoxin levels ~2×.
pairs.recommendation: Halve digoxin dose; monitor digoxin level + ECG.

pairs.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition → statin rhabdomyolysis risk.
pairs.recommendation: Hold statin during macrolide course, or switch to azithromycin.

pairs.mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition → simvastatin AUC up to 3–7×.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit; or switch to pravastatin/rosuvastatin.

pairs.mechanism: Acute/binge alcohol raises lactic acidosis risk.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid binge drinking; moderate intake acceptable with meals.

pairs.mechanism: Contrast-induced AKI + metformin accumulation → lactic acidosis.
pairs.recommendation: Hold metformin at time of contrast; resume 48 h after, if eGFR stable.

pairs.mechanism: Alcohol impairs hepatic gluconeogenesis → delayed hypoglycaemia.
pairs.recommendation: Advise food with alcohol; check glucose before sleep.

pairs.mechanism: Calcium binds T4 in gut, reduces absorption ~30%.
pairs.recommendation: Separate doses by >=4 h.

pairs.mechanism: Iron binds T4 in gut, reduces absorption.
pairs.recommendation: Separate doses by >=4 h.

pairs.mechanism: Additive myelosuppression; CYP3A4-mediated PK interactions with many cytotoxic regimens.
pairs.recommendation: Do not combine without oncologist supervision.

pairs.mechanism: Unknown effect on tumour clearance; potential additive toxicity.
pairs.recommendation: Defer elective senolytic protocols until chemotherapy washout (>=4 wk) unless on clinical trial.

pairs.mechanism: Dasatinib may affect CYP3A4 and platelet function.
pairs.recommendation: Monitor INR and platelets during senolytic pulse.

pairs.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces contraceptive levels; pregnancy risk.
pairs.recommendation: Use alternative contraception or stop St John's wort.

pairs.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition raises amiodarone AUC.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit with amiodarone.

pairs.mechanism: In-vitro CYP2C9 inhibition; possible INR rise at high doses.
pairs.recommendation: Monitor INR if quercetin >500 mg/d is added.

pairs.mechanism: Mild additive antiplatelet effect at high fish-oil doses.
pairs.recommendation: Clinically insignificant at <1 g/d fish oil.

pairs.mechanism: Additive antiplatelet effect.
pairs.recommendation: Avoid in patients with bleeding history; monitor otherwise.
